name: dumbbell burpee
instructions: Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand.
Lower your body into a squat position, placing the dumbbells on the ground in front of you.
Kick your feet back into a push-up position, keeping your body in a straight line.
Perform a push-up, bending your elbows and lowering your chest towards the ground.
Jump your feet back towards your hands, landing in a squat position.
Stand up explosively, lifting the dumbbells off the ground and bringing them to your shoulders.
Press the dumbbells overhead, fully extending your arms.
Lower the dumbbells back to your shoulders and repeat the entire sequence for the desired number of repetitions.

Start in a standing position with your feet shoulder-width apart and a dumbbell in each hand. Lower your body into a squat position, placing the dumbbells on the ground in front of you. Kick your feet back into a push-up position, keeping your body in a straight line. Perform a push-up, bending your elbows and lowering your chest towards the ground. Jump your feet back towards your hands, landing in a squat position. Stand up explosively, lifting the dumbbells off the ground and bringing them to your shoulders. Press the dumbbells overhead, fully extending your arms. Lower the dumbbells back to your shoulders and repeat the entire sequence for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
